metadata.synopsis: The less-than-appealing podiatrist, Dr. Heckyl, invents a potion that turns him into Mr. Hype, a handsome, charismatic lady killer - literally!
metadata.title: Dr. Heckyl & Mr. Hype

metadata.synopsis: When a gang of alien-clowns land in a nearby area (in a ship shaped like a circus big-top), the cops are skeptical. But before long, there can be no doubt: Killer Klowns from Outer Space are here to turn the Earth's population into candy floss!
metadata.title: Killer Klowns From Outer Space

metadata.synopsis: In this cult-classic zom-com, two totally awesome Valley Girls (Catherine Mary Stewart, Kelli Maroney) kick some zombie butt and go on the lam from bloodthirsty scientists after a passing comet turns all of Los Angeles into the walking undead!
metadata.title: Night Of The Comet

metadata.synopsis: Unaware the entire citizenry has been replaced by aliens, a professor arrives in a Midwestern town looking for his ex-wife who vanished.
metadata.title: Strange Invaders

metadata.synopsis: In this thrilling creature feature, a young boy’s life is turned upside-down when alien invaders intent on taking over the world land in his backyard!
metadata.title: Invaders From Mars

metadata.synopsis: An early 1960s Mexican western in the vaquero style. Recently widowed ranch owner Gabriela Bermúdez is facing trouble from neighboring haciendado Don Atenor. Her aunt, Tia Lola, tells her this is why she should remarry and Gabriela reluctantly accepts the notion. She decides he should be ranch savvy and strong and goes to the town fair to peruse the bravado and deeds of the macho men hanging out. Agustin and his buddy El Tigre provide comic relief, as Tigre falls in love with Gabriela, though he is not really her type. Madly in love he tries to impress her in increasingly comic situations, eventually taking on the guise of a gun runner to seem more worldly and able to handle situations. Director Julian Soler does a good job of balancing the light and serious sides of the movie making the production an enjoyable example of early 1960s Mexican vaquero movies. The tradition of the American cowboy, is the subject matter of countless Hollywood movies. But those movies have never dealt with the true origins with the Mexican "vaquero" whose traditions were adapted to create the iconic American "Cowboy", originally a truly Mexican tradition.
metadata.title: Escuela de valientes
